title: Run the spike across 4 frontier models and capture results
description:
# Run the spike across 4 frontier models

## What

Execute the harness against all curated tasks × all models, capture results, organize for analysis.

## Models

- claude-4.6-sonnet (Anthropic)
- claude-4.7-opus (Anthropic)
- gpt-5.3-codex (OpenAI)
- gemini-3.1-pro (Google)

(Substitute current frontier-equivalent if any of these are unavailable when the spike runs.)

## What to record

Per task × model pair:
- All metrics from the harness output (tokens, latency, cost, success).
- Full transcripts (prompt, response, tool invocations, Lua source generated, runtime results).

## Storage

Results land under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/results/<run-id>/<model>/<task>.json` plus an aggregate summary at `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/results/<run-id>/summary.csv`.

## Cost budget for the spike itself

The spike makes real API calls. Each model × task pair is small (<$0.20). Across ~15 tasks × 4 models, expect ~$10–20 total. Hard cap: $50 across the spike. If approaching, halt and reassess.

## Deliverable

A `run-id` results directory with all transcripts, a summary CSV, and confirmation that the harness completed all combinations (or documented exclusions if any model was unavailable / refused / errored systematically).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Blocked on real-provider credentials/configuration.

Readiness command run:
```bash
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py --check-providers
```

Output summary:
- `anthropic` package present, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` missing, ready=false.
- `openai` package present, `OPENAI_API_KEY` missing, ready=false.
- `litellm` package present, no supported provider credential env vars present (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `VERTEXAI_PROJECT`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` all missing), ready=false.

No real-provider calls were run, preserving the spike cost cap. The harness is ready for a one-task provider readiness run once credentials are configured, for example:

```bash
python spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py \
  --task predict_single_item \
  --model litellm:<configured-low-cost-model> \
  --run-id provider-readiness-001
```

Stub smoke still passes 15/15 with `stub-oracle`.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added enforceable pre-dispatch cost planning to the spike harness.

Changes:
- `harness.py` now has default spike cost controls:
  - `--max-total-cost-usd` default `50.0`
  - `--estimated-real-call-cost-usd` default `0.20`
- Before dispatching any selected run, the harness computes:
  - real provider call count = non-stub model count × task count
  - estimated real-provider cost = real provider call count × per-call estimate
- If estimated cost exceeds the cap, the harness exits before provider readiness checks or paid calls.
- Each run now writes `run_metadata.json` with selected tasks, selected models, boot-prompt token count, and the cost plan.
- `report.py` now includes the run cost plan when metadata exists.
- README documents the default `$50` cap and `$0.20` per-real-call estimate and the explicit override flags.

Verification:
```bash
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --models stub-oracle --run-id ignored-cost-report-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --model litellm:dummy-model --max-total-cost-usd 0.10 --estimated-real-call-cost-usd 0.20 --run-id should-not-run 2>&1 || true
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py" --run-id ignored-cost-report-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" --run-id ignored-cost-report-verify
```

Results:
- Stub run succeeded and wrote metadata.
- Over-cap real-provider plan was refused before any provider call.
- Report generated with catalog measurement and cost plan.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Added an explicit readiness-run guard before full real-provider matrix runs.

Changes:
- New CLI flag: `--readiness-run-id`.
- When `--all` includes any real provider model, the harness now requires:
  - `--allow-full-matrix`
  - `--readiness-run-id <run-id>` pointing to an existing `results/<run-id>/summary.csv`
  - that summary must contain at least one successful non-`stub-oracle` result
- Stub-only full runs are unaffected.
- README full-matrix examples now include `--readiness-run-id` and document the requirement.

Tests added:
- missing readiness run id is rejected
- missing readiness summary is rejected
- stub-only readiness success is rejected
- successful real-provider readiness row is accepted

Verification:
```bash
python -m pytest "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py" -q
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --models litellm:dummy-model --allow-full-matrix --run-id should-not-run 2>&1 || true
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-readiness-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py" --run-id ignored-readiness-verify
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" --run-id ignored-readiness-verify
```

Results:
- Guardrail tests: 19 passed.
- Full real-provider matrix without readiness run id was refused before provider calls.
- Stub oracle full run still passed 15/15.
- Catalog measurement and report generation still work.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Ran a one-task OpenAI readiness check with GPT 5.4 mini after `.env` provider readiness was available.

Command:
```bash
set -a && source .env && set +a && python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --model openai:gpt-5.4-mini --run-id provider-readiness-openai-gpt54mini-001
```

Result:
- Completed provider call successfully.
- Strict checker result: 0/1 passed.
- Failure classification: `boot_prompt`.
- Provider-reported usage: 830 input tokens, 241 output tokens.
- Stub Plexus cost spent: `$0.0168`.

Generated Lua behavior:
- Correctly used `require("plexus")`.
- Correctly called `plexus.score.info` and `plexus.score.predict`.
- Returned predicted value, explanation, score version id, and `total_cost`.

Why the checker failed:
- The task prompt asks for predicted value, explanation, score version id, and total cost.
- The task definition/checker also requires `item_id`, `score_id`, `cost`, and a `plexus.item.info` call.
- GPT 5.4 mini did not call `plexus.item.info`, did not include `item_id`/`score_id`, and used `total_cost` rather than `cost`.

This is useful signal: the model can generate executable Lua for the simple task, but the spike task prompt is underspecified relative to its expected_outcome/required_apis. Next unblocked improvement is to align the prompt with the checker (or deliberately relax the checker if the stricter fields are not actually required).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Ran a one-task OpenAI readiness check with GPT 5.4.

Command:
```bash
set -a && source .env && set +a && python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --model openai:gpt-5.4 --run-id provider-readiness-openai-gpt54-001
```

Result:
- Completed provider call successfully.
- Strict checker result: 0/1 passed.
- Failure classification: `boot_prompt`.
- Provider-reported usage: 830 input tokens, 336 output tokens.
- Stub Plexus cost spent: `$0.0168`.

Generated Lua behavior:
- Used `require("plexus")`.
- Generated executable Lua.
- Found Compliance Tone by listing scorecards and scorecard details.
- Called `plexus.score.predict` and returned predicted value, explanation, score version id, and `total_cost`.

Why the checker failed:
- Same core prompt/checker mismatch seen with GPT 5.4 mini and GPT 5.3 Codex.
- The task prompt asks for predicted value, explanation, score version id, and total cost.
- The checker also requires `plexus.score.info`, `plexus.item.info`, returned `item_id`, returned `score_id`, and a field named `cost`.
- GPT 5.4 used scorecard discovery instead of `plexus.score.info`, did not call `plexus.item.info`, and returned `total_cost` instead of `cost`.

Interpretation:
- GPT 5.4 can generate runnable Tactus Lua for this task, but the current task is underspecified relative to its strict expected outcome. This should be fixed before treating this first-task readiness as a model failure.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Completed the Prompt Contract Repair Plan and reran GPT 5.4.

Changes made:
- Updated `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/tasks/03_predict_single_item.yaml` so the prompt explicitly asks for:
  - `plexus.score.info{ id = "score_compliance_tone" }`
  - `plexus.item.info{ id = "item_1007" }`
  - `plexus.score.predict`
  - return fields `item_id`, `score_id`, `score_version_id`, `predicted_value`, `explanation`, and `cost`
- Updated `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/boot_prompt.md` prediction example to inspect score/item first and return `score_id` plus `cost`.
- Added a prompt/checker consistency test for `predict_single_item`.

Local verification:
```bash
python -m pytest "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/test_harness_report.py" -q
python -m py_compile "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/plexus_module_stub.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/report.py" "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/measure_mcp_catalog.py"
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --validate-tasks
python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --all --model stub-oracle --run-id ignored-prompt-contract-verify
```
Results:
- Guardrail tests: 20 passed.
- Boot prompt: 810 / 2000 estimated tokens.
- Stub oracle: 15/15 passed.

Paid rerun:
```bash
set -a && source .env && set +a && python "spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/harness.py" --task predict_single_item --model openai:gpt-5.4 --run-id provider-readiness-openai-gpt54-promptfix-001
```
Result:
- Strict checker: 0/1 passed.
- Failure classification: `language`.
- Usage: 938 input tokens, 257 output tokens.
- The model followed the repaired contract in content, but duplicated the Lua program back-to-back. The second `local plexus = require("plexus")` after the first `return` caused `LuaSyntaxError: <eof> expected near 'local'`.

Interpretation:
- The hidden prompt/checker mismatch is fixed for this task.
- The next blocker is exactly the feedback-loop issue: syntactic validation should catch this and return a clear repair message so the model can remove the duplicated program.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Post-contract readiness executed with openai:gpt-5.4-mini. New runs: readiness-gpt54mini-async-budget-001, readiness-gpt54mini-report-async-budget-001, and provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-budgetcontract-005. Async budget contract held in generated Tactus for evaluation/report async calls. Full pass currently 14/15 with one boot_prompt-classified miss on false_negative_feedback_summary (representative_item_id + item.info usage).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: After tightening false_negative_feedback_summary prompt/checker, targeted rerun readiness-gpt54mini-fn-summary-promptfix-001 passed. Full rerun provider-full-openai-gpt54-mini-budgetcontract-006 now passes 15/15 first-try with repair_attempts=0. This removes the prior boot_prompt-classified miss observed in run -005.
